note: Attribute values are end-game targets (level 60, all 5 attributes start at 3). Invest your primary attribute first — most perks and the strongest cyberware are gated behind attribute thresholds. Swap iconic weapons for whatever you find; the archetype is what matters.

builds.name: Quickhack Netrunner
builds.summary: Kill whole rooms without firing a shot. Spread Contagion + Overheat through a crowd, finish with Synapse Burnout, and let RAM regen do the rest. The most forgiving high-damage build in the game on any difficulty.
builds.attrNote: Intelligence is everything — it scales quickhack damage, RAM and cyberdeck slots. Cool adds crit + the headhunter quickhacks; the rest is survivability.

picks: Spillover — combat quickhacks spread to nearby enemies, turning single targets into AoE
Embedded Exploit — quickhacking a target builds a stack that detonates as extra damage
Queen of Hearts — big damage bonus vs. high-health enemies
Bartmoss' Legacy — Ultimate quickhacks cost far less RAM, so you can chain Burnouts
Ninjutsu / Crouching Tiger — sneak-attack and crouch bonuses for the opener
Deadeye + Killer Instinct — crit chance/damage that also boosts smart-gun cleanup

cyberware.why: Lets a quickhack spread and lowers the cost of the next hack of the same type — the netrunner engine.
Mechatronic Core supercharges Ultimate quickhacks; Camillo refunds RAM on a hacked kill so you never run dry.
Unlocks smart weapons as a reliable backup to clean up survivors.
Free revive and an auto-heal at low HP — you're squishy, so insure the body.
Cloak to reposition while hacks tick and RAM recovers.

weapons.name: Yinglong (Smart AR)
weapons.why: Homing rounds finish anything that survives the hacks — no aiming required.

weapons.name: Problem Solver (Smart SMG)
weapons.why: Locks onto multiple targets at once for crowd cleanup.

playstyle: Ping a target to reveal the whole network, then upload Contagion or Overheat — Spillover carries it across the room.
Detonate clusters with Synapse Burnout / Cyberpsychosis; reposition with Optical Camo while RAM regenerates.
Stay at range and in cover — your power is the deck, not your gun.
builds.leveling: Intelligence to 20 first (unlock Spillover/Bartmoss early), then Cool to 15, then spread the rest into Body/Reflexes/Tech for survivability.
cons: RAM-dependent — weak if caught with an empty deck
Less satisfying for players who want gunplay

builds.name: Sandevistan Katana
builds.summary: The Edgerunner fantasy. Freeze time with a Sandevistan and dice an entire room with a katana before anyone reacts. Highest skill-expression melee build, absurd burst.
builds.attrNote: Reflexes drives blade damage and crit; Cool drives crit damage, sneak damage and the best Sandevistan perks. Body keeps you alive in melee range.

picks: Bladerunner — blade damage scales with how fast you're moving
Stuck Pig + Bloodlust — bleeding enemies take more, and you heal on blade hits
Float like a Butterfly / Dash — mobility to close gaps and dodge-cancel
Deadeye — convert crouch/standstill into massive crit
Style over Substance / Killer Instinct — crit chance + damage during Sandevistan
Feared by the Dead — kills extend cooldown recovery

cyberware.why: Best slow-mo: long duration, big crit and crit-damage bonus while active — the core of the build.
Optional second melee — strong gap-closer and execute when the katana's on cooldown.
Heal as you take cyberware segments + mitigation to survive the dive.
A second mini slow-mo when you dodge/aim with a blade — extends your time-stop windows.
Raw armor and shorter debuffs — you're taking hits up close.

weapons.name: Satori (Iconic Katana)
weapons.why: Guaranteed crit against enemies at full health — perfect for opening a fight in Sandevistan.

weapons.name: Errata / Tsumetogi (Katana)
weapons.why: Tech/cool katanas with bleed and crit for sustained fights.

playstyle: Activate Sandevistan, sprint into the densest group, and chain light attacks — everything is frozen.
Dodge-cancel to reset Kerenzikov and keep crit windows up; deflect bullets mid-Sandy.
Manage the meter — kill, drop out of slow-mo, let it recharge behind cover, repeat.
builds.leveling: Reflexes to ~15 early for blade perks, push Cool for crit, then both to 20; trickle into Body for HP.
pros: Insane burst — clears rooms in one window
cons: Melee range is risky without the meter
Punishing if you mistime the Sandevistan cooldown

builds.name: Shotgun Berserk Tank
builds.summary: Walk into the room and walk out. Berserk reduces damage and steadies your aim while you delete enemies point-blank with a shotgun and stack Adrenaline as a second health bar.
builds.attrNote: Body is the spine — Adrenaline (overshield), health and shotgun/melee damage. Technical pushes armor and unlocks Edgerunner so you can overload on chrome.

picks: Adrenaline Rush + Buffer Buildup — kills bank a growing overshield
Pain to Gain — heal a chunk of the damage you deal
Hard Hitter / Bushwhack — shotgun and blunt damage, bonus vs. dazed targets
Unshakable + Juggernaut — keep firing through hits, mitigation while Adrenaline is up
Edgerunner — exceed your cyberware capacity for a huge power spike
All Things Cyber + License to Chrome — more capacity and cheaper, stronger cyberware

cyberware.why: Damage reduction, recoil control and melee/health bonuses — turns you into a wall.
Stacks raw armor and cuts debuff duration — the tank core.
Revive insurance and even more overshield uptime.
Heal on segments + carry/health for the brawl.
Boosts Power-weapon ricochet shots for trick-shot shotgun kills.

weapons.name: Carnage / Zhuo (Power & Tech Shotgun)
weapons.why: Carnage two-shots at range; Zhuo charges through cover. Either deletes targets up close.

weapons.name: Defender (LMG)
weapons.why: Suppressing fire for when you want to stay back and tank rounds.

weapons.name: Gorilla Arms
weapons.why: Berserk-boosted punches stagger and execute; great for finishing dazed enemies.

playstyle: Pop Berserk, push the front line, and stack Adrenaline kills to build your overshield.
Stay aggressive — Pain to Gain heals you faster than you take damage when you keep killing.
Use Gorilla Arms on heavies/MaxTac to break their guard, then finish with the shotgun.
builds.leveling: Body to 20 first, then Technical to 18 (rush Edgerunner once your capacity can take it); dip Reflexes for handling.
cons: Short range — struggles vs. snipers/turrets at distance

builds.name: Smart-Gun Tech Engineer
builds.summary: Let the bullets do the aiming. Smart weapons home onto tagged enemies while charged tech weapons punch through walls and cover — a hybrid that softens with quickhacks and finishes with hardware.
builds.attrNote: Technical maxes cyberware capacity and tech-weapon damage; Intelligence powers the cyberdeck for tagging and softening. Cool adds crit for the smart-gun cleanup.

picks: Edgerunner — go over capacity for a massive cyberware power spike
Lickety Split — tech weapons charge faster and hit harder
Built Different / Renaissance Punk — flat damage based on attributes invested
Embedded Exploit + Spillover — tag and soften groups for the smart guns
Ping → Weapon Glitch — light up the whole room and debuff their weapons

cyberware.why: Mandatory — unlocks smart-weapon target tracking. This build is built around it.
Slots utility quickhacks (Ping, Weapon Glitch, Reboot Optics) to mark and blind targets.
Improves smart-link lock speed and weak-spot detection.
Stays alive at mid-range while the guns track.
Double jump for high firing angles over cover.

weapons.name: Yinglong / Divided We Stand (Smart AR)
weapons.why: Locks onto multiple targets and curves rounds around cover.

weapons.name: Nekomata (Tech Sniper)
weapons.why: Charge a shot through walls to one-tap snipers and heavies you've tagged.

weapons.name: Ashura (Smart Sniper)
weapons.why: Smart sniper that tracks tagged enemies at long range.

playstyle: Ping to reveal and tag, optionally Weapon Glitch the dangerous shooters, then hold the trigger and let smart rounds track.
Switch to the charged tech sniper for armoured targets and through-wall kills.
Stay at medium-long range; reposition with the double jump.
builds.leveling: Technical to 20 (Edgerunner + tech-weapon perks), then Intelligence to 18, then Cool to 12.
pros: Forgiving aim — guns track for you

builds.name: Revolver Gunslinger
builds.summary: One bullet, one kill. Stack crit and headshot multipliers on revolvers and power pistols for one-shot bodyshots — the highest single-target ceiling in the game for players with good aim.
builds.attrNote: Reflexes scales handgun damage and crit chance; Cool scales crit damage, headshot damage and steadier aim. A Sandevistan is optional for buying aim time.

picks: Gunslinger — faster reload/draw on handguns
Long Shot Draw — charge a handgun shot for huge bonus damage
Lead and Steel / Submission — handgun crit and stagger
Deadeye — convert standstill into crit damage
Steady Hand + Quick Draw — reduce sway and ready the next shot instantly
Headhunter — stacking bonus damage on weak-spot hits

cyberware.why: Slow-mo at low HP and on aim — buys the split-second you need to land headshots.
Optional slow-mo so you can place precision shots; swap to Berserk for recoil if you prefer raw stats.
Predicts moving targets and boosts headshot reliability.
Insurance for a glass-cannon that wants to stay mobile.
Mitigation so a stray hit doesn't end a run.

weapons.name: Comrade's Hammer (Iconic Tech Revolver)
weapons.why: Charged explosive round that one-shots almost anything — the gunslinger's hammer.

weapons.name: La Chingona Dorada / Overture
weapons.why: Fast-firing power revolvers that ricochet for trick-shot crits.

weapons.name: Malorian Arms 3516 (Iconic)
weapons.why: Johnny's smart pistol — huge damage and pure Cyberpunk flavour.

playstyle: Open from stealth with a charged headshot (Deadeye + Long Shot Draw) for an instant kill.
Slow time with Reflex Tuner/Sandevistan when swarmed and pick off heads one by one.
Reposition between shots — you hit like a truck but can't trade sustained fire.
builds.leveling: Reflexes to 20 first for handgun scaling, then Cool to 18 for crit/headshot, then Body to 12.
cons: Weak crowd control without the Sandy

builds.name: Ghost Sniper / Netrunner
builds.summary: Clear the map and they never knew you were there. Cloak, breach the network, soften with quickhacks, and pick off the rest with silenced tech snipers and sneak takedowns.
builds.attrNote: Cool maxes stealth, sneak-attack and crit damage; Intelligence powers Breach Protocol and combat quickhacks to thin a base before you fire. Reflexes adds sniper handling.

picks: Ninjutsu — buffed sneak attacks and non-lethal takedowns
Cutthroat + Feared by the Dead — stronger stealth strikes and post-kill stealth bonuses
Deadeye + Headhunter — turn the first sniper shot into an instant kill
Breach Protocol mastery (Compression, Total Recall) — longer, cheaper daemon uploads
Embedded Exploit — soften the whole network before the first shot

cyberware.why: On-demand invisibility to reposition and break line of sight — the stealth core.
Spread Reboot Optics / Cyberpsychosis to neutralise cameras and lone guards quietly.
Silent movement and a double jump for sniper perches.
Slow-mo when you're detected — buys time to re-cloak or land the kill.
See enemy cones and tag everyone through walls before you move.

weapons.name: Breakthrough (Iconic Tech Sniper)
weapons.why: Silenced charged shots punch through walls for one-tap stealth kills.

weapons.name: Nekomata (Tech Sniper)
weapons.why: Through-cover charge sniper that pairs with tagging.

weapons.name: Mantis Blades
weapons.why: Silent leap-takedowns to clear isolated guards without a sound.

playstyle: Breach the access point on arrival to upload daemons across the network, then Reboot Optics on cameras.
Cloak, reposition to a perch, and chain charged headshots — re-cloak before the timer runs out.
Use quickhacks (Cyberpsychosis, Suicide) to make guards remove each other quietly.
builds.leveling: Cool to 20 for stealth/crit, Intelligence to 18 for Breach + quickhacks, Reflexes to 12 for sniper handling.
cons: Very low HP — one mistake hurts
